0.original: At a homecoming celebration last year, I met a college friend whom I had not seen for ten years.
0.b1: At a school reunion last year, I met a college friend I hadn't seen for ten years.
0.cn: 去年在校友聚会上，我见到了十年没见的一位大学朋友。
0.ai_notes: 'homecoming': a yearly tradition in American schools and colleges where former students return to visit their old campus.
0.remarks: 'homecoming': 美国学校和大学的一项年度传统，校友们会回到母校参观。

1.original: Chuck was a very bright student and was graduated with honors.
1.b1: Chuck was a very smart student and graduated with high marks.
1.cn: 查克是个非常聪明的学生，以优异的成绩毕业。
1.ai_notes: 'graduated with honors': finished school with high grades or special recognition.
1.remarks: 'graduated with honors': 以优异成绩或获得特殊表彰毕业。

2.original: His goal when I last saw him was to own his own business in western Nebraska.
2.b1: When I last saw him, his goal was to start his own business in western Nebraska.
2.cn: 我上次见到他时，他的目标是在内布拉斯加州西部开创自己的事业。
2.ai_notes: 'western Nebraska': a specific region in the United States; it is often rural and less populated than the eastern part of the state.
2.remarks: 'western Nebraska': 美国的一个特定地区；通常比该州东部更偏远、人口更少。

3.original: I asked Chuck what kind of business he finally established.
3.b1: I asked Chuck what kind of business he finally started.
3.cn: 我问查克他最终创办了什么样的生意。
3.ai_notes: 'established': to start or create something, like a business or an organization, that is intended to last for a long time.
3.remarks: 'established': 创办或建立旨在长期存在的事物，如企业或组织。

4.original: Well, he confessed, I didn't go into business for myself.
4.b1: Well, he admitted, I didn't start my own business.
4.cn: 嗯，他坦白说，我并没有自己创业。
4.ai_notes: 'go into business for myself': a common phrase meaning to start one's own company rather than working for someone else.
4.remarks: 'go into business for myself': 一个常用短语，意为自己创业，而不是为他人工作。

5.original: I wouldn't have said this to anyone five years ago or even one year ago,
5.b1: I wouldn't have told this to anyone five years ago or even one year ago,
5.cn: 五年前甚至一年前，我都不会对任何人说这些，
5.ai_notes: 'I wouldn't have said this': a conditional structure used to talk about a past action that did not happen.
5.remarks: 'I wouldn't have said this': 一种条件句结构，用于谈论过去未曾发生的事情。

6.original: but now I'm ready to talk about it.
6.b1: but now I am ready to talk about it.
6.cn: 但现在我准备好谈谈这件事了。
6.ai_notes: 'ready to talk about it': prepared to discuss a personal or sensitive topic openly.
6.remarks: 'ready to talk about it': 准备好公开讨论某个个人或敏感话题。

7.original: As I look back at my college education now, I see that I became an expert in why a business idea won't work out.
7.b1: Looking back at my education, I realize I became an expert in why a business idea will fail.
7.cn: 现在回首我的大学教育，我发现我成了研究商业想法为何会失败的专家。
7.ai_notes: 'look back at': to think about or reflect on something that happened in the past.
7.remarks: 'look back at': 回顾或反思过去发生的事情。

8.original: I learned every conceivable pitfall, every reason why a small business will fail.
8.b1: I learned every possible problem and every reason why a small business fails.
8.cn: 我学会了所有可能遇到的陷阱，以及小企业失败的每一个原因。
8.ai_notes: 'conceivable': something that is possible to imagine or understand.

9.original: You've got to have ample capital.
9.cn: 你必须拥有充足的资金。
9.ai_notes: 'ample capital': having enough money or financial resources to start and maintain a business.
9.remarks: 'ample capital': 拥有足够的资金或财务资源来创办和维持企业。

10.original: Be sure the business cycle is right.
10.b1: Make sure the timing for the business is right.
10.cn: 确保商业周期是合适的。
10.ai_notes: 'business cycle': the natural rise and fall of economic growth over time.
10.remarks: 'business cycle': 经济增长随时间自然出现的起伏波动。

11.original: Is there a big demand for what you will offer?
11.b1: Is there a high demand for your product?
11.cn: 人们对你提供的产品有很大的需求吗？
11.ai_notes: 'demand': the amount of desire or need that customers have for a specific product or service.
11.remarks: 'demand': 客户对特定产品或服务的渴望或需求量。

12.ai_notes: 'stabilized': to become steady and unlikely to change or fail suddenly.
12.remarks: 'stabilized': 变得稳定，不太可能突然改变或失败。

13.original: A thousand and one things to check out.
13.b1: There are so many things to check.
13.cn: 有一千零一件事情需要核实。
13.ai_notes: 'a thousand and one': an idiom used to emphasize that there is a very large or endless number of things.
13.remarks: 'a thousand and one': 一个习语，用于强调数量非常多或无穷无尽。

14.original: The thing that hurts most is that several of my old high school friends,
14.b1: What hurts the most is that several of my old high school friends,
14.cn: 最让人难受的是，我以前的几个高中同学，
14.ai_notes: 'hurts most': the thing that causes the greatest amount of emotional pain or disappointment.
14.remarks: 'hurts most': 造成最大情感痛苦或失望的事情。

15.original: who never seemed to have much on the ball and didn't even go to college,
15.b1: who didn't seem very smart and didn't even go to college,
15.cn: 他们看起来并不怎么聪明，甚至没上过大学，
15.ai_notes: 'have much on the ball': an idiom meaning to be clever, capable, or efficient at doing things.
15.remarks: 'have much on the ball': 一个习语，意为聪明、能干或办事效率高。

16.original: now are very well established in their own businesses.
16.b1: are now very successful in their own businesses.
16.cn: 现在却在各自的生意上取得了很大的成就。
16.ai_notes: 'well established': successful and secure in a position or business.
16.remarks: 'well established': 在职位或业务上取得成功且稳固。

17.ai_notes: 'But me?': a short, informal way to shift the focus of a conversation to oneself.
17.remarks: 'But me?': 一种简短、非正式的方式，用于将谈话焦点转移到自己身上。

18.original: I'm just plodding along, auditing freight shipments.
18.b1: I am just working slowly, checking freight shipments.
18.cn: 我只是在按部就班地工作，审核货运单。
18.ai_notes: 'plodding along': working slowly and steadily, often in a boring or unexciting way.
18.remarks: 'plodding along': 缓慢而稳定地工作，通常显得枯燥或乏味。

19.original: Had I been drilled a little more in why a small business can succeed,
19.b1: If I had been taught more about how a small business can succeed,
19.cn: 如果我当时多学一点关于小企业如何成功的内容，
19.ai_notes: 'drilled': to be taught something repeatedly through intense practice or instruction.
19.remarks: 'drilled': 通过高强度的练习或指导反复教授某事。

20.original: I'd be better off in every way today.
20.b1: I would be in a much better position today.
20.cn: 我今天在各方面都会过得更好。
20.ai_notes: 'better off': in a more favorable or successful situation than before.
20.remarks: 'better off': 处于比以前更有利或更成功的境地。

21.original: The thinking that guided Chuck's intelligence was a lot more important than the amount of Chuck's intelligence.
21.b1: The way Chuck thought was more important than how smart he was.
21.cn: 引导查克智慧的思维方式，比查克本身的聪明程度重要得多。
21.ai_notes: 'guided': influenced or directed by a specific force or idea.
21.remarks: 'guided': 受特定力量或思想的影响或引导。

22.original: Why some brilliant people are failures.
22.b1: Why some very smart people fail.
22.cn: 为什么一些聪明人会失败。

23.original: I've been close for many years to a person who qualifies as a genius,
23.b1: I have been close for many years to a person who is a genius,
23.cn: 多年来，我一直和一个称得上是天才的人很亲近，
23.ai_notes: 'qualifies as': meets the necessary standards to be considered something.
23.remarks: 'qualifies as': 符合必要标准，被视为某事物。

24.original: has high abstract intelligence, and is Phi Beta Kappa.
24.b1: he has high intelligence and was a top student.
24.cn: 他有很高的抽象思维能力，还是优等生协会的成员。
24.ai_notes: 'Phi Beta Kappa': a prestigious American honor society for university students who have achieved high academic excellence.
24.remarks: 'Phi Beta Kappa': 美国一个著名的大学荣誉学会，授予学术成就优异的学生。

25.original: Despite this very high native intelligence, he is one of the most unsuccessful people I know.
25.b1: Even with this high intelligence, he is one of the most unsuccessful people I know.
25.cn: 尽管有这种与生俱来的高智商，他却是我认识的最不成功的人之一。
25.ai_notes: 'native intelligence': the natural mental ability a person is born with, rather than what they learned in school.
25.remarks: 'native intelligence': 一个人天生的智力，而非后天学校所学。

26.original: He has a very mediocre job.
26.b1: He has a very average job.
26.cn: 他有一份很平庸的工作。
26.ai_notes: 'mediocre': average or below average; not very good.
26.remarks: 'mediocre': 平庸或低于平均水平；不太好。

27.b1: He is afraid of taking responsibility.
27.ai_notes: 'responsibility': the state of having a duty to deal with something or having control over someone.
27.remarks: 'responsibility': 处理某事的义务或对某人的控制权。

28.ai_notes: 'never married': a simple way to state that someone has remained single throughout their life.
28.remarks: 'never married': 简单陈述某人终身未婚。

29.original: Lots of marriages end in divorce.
29.cn: 许多婚姻最终以离婚告终。
29.ai_notes: 'end in divorce': a common way to describe a marriage that has legally finished.
29.remarks: 'end in divorce': 描述婚姻在法律上结束的常用方式。

30.ai_notes: 'few friends': implies that the person has almost no friends, which is a negative observation.
30.remarks: 'few friends': 暗示某人几乎没有朋友，这是一种负面观察。

31.ai_notes: 'bore him': to make someone feel uninterested or tired because of a lack of excitement.
31.remarks: 'bore him': 因为缺乏趣味而使某人感到无聊或疲倦。

32.original: He's never invested in property of any kind.
32.b1: He has never invested in any property.
32.cn: 他从未投资过任何房产。
32.ai_notes: 'invested in property': to buy land or buildings with the hope of making more money later.
32.remarks: 'invested in property': 购买土地或房产，希望未来能获利。

33.b1: He is afraid he might lose his money.
33.cn: 他担心自己可能会赔钱。
33.ai_notes: 'might': used here to express a fear or possibility of a negative outcome.
33.remarks: 'might': 在此处用于表达对负面结果的担忧或可能性。

34.original: This man uses his great brain power to prove why things won't work,
34.b1: This man uses his great brain to prove why things will fail,
34.cn: 这个人用他强大的大脑去证明事情为什么行不通，
34.ai_notes: 'brain power': the ability to think clearly and solve problems.
34.remarks: 'brain power': 清晰思考和解决问题的能力。

35.original: rather than directing his mental power to searching for ways to succeed.
35.b1: instead of using his mind to find ways to succeed.
35.cn: 而不是把他的脑力用来寻找成功的方法。
35.ai_notes: 'rather than': instead of; used to show a preference for one thing over another.
35.remarks: 'rather than': 而不是；用于表示对某事物的偏好。

36.original: Because of the negative thinking that guides his great reservoir of brains, this fellow contributes little and creates nothing.
36.b1: Because of his negative thinking, he contributes little and creates nothing.
36.cn: 由于引导他丰富大脑的是消极思维，这家伙贡献很少，也创造不出什么。
36.ai_notes: 'reservoir of brains': a metaphor suggesting the person has a large, deep supply of intelligence that is not being used.
36.remarks: 'reservoir of brains': 一个比喻，暗示某人拥有大量未被利用的深层智慧。

37.original: With a changed attitude, he could do great things indeed.
37.b1: With a different attitude, he could really do great things.
37.cn: 如果态度改变，他确实可以成就伟大的事业。
37.ai_notes: 'indeed': used to emphasize a statement or confirm that something is true.
37.remarks: 'indeed': 用于强调陈述或确认某事属实。

38.original: He has the brains to be a tremendous success, but not the thought power.
38.b1: He has the intelligence to be very successful, but not the right way of thinking.
38.cn: 他有取得巨大成功的智力，但缺乏正确的思维能力。
38.ai_notes: 'tremendous success': a very high level of achievement or victory.
38.remarks: 'tremendous success': 极高水平的成就或胜利。

39.original: Another person I know well was inducted into the Army shortly after earning a Ph.D.
39.b1: Another person I know well joined the Army shortly after getting his Ph.D.
39.cn: 我认识的另一个人在获得博士学位后不久就应征入伍了。
39.ai_notes: 'inducted into the Army': officially entered into military service.
39.remarks: 'inducted into the Army': 正式入伍。
